campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review how the city's money was spent and invested in July 2025. They also discussed plans to improve transportation options around Campbell, including ways to make it easier for people to get around using different methods like buses, biking, and walking.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment and spending reports
City's cash and financial position update
New multimodal transportation plan (buses, bikes, walking, cars)

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents could comment on a proposed new rule or regulation. After hearing public input, the council moved forward by introducing the ordinance and skipping the standard first reading process to speed things up. The ordinance is now scheduled to be officially adopted at the next council meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for resident input and feedback
New ordinance introduced and first reading waived (fast-tracked)
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption at next regular council meeting

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from various sessions held in March and to authorize a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The council reviewed records from multiple special meetings and study sessions to ensure they were accurate before moving forward with approving a construction project.
keyTopics: Approval of March meeting minutes from multiple council sessions
Authorization of construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Review of closed session and special joint study session records

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 15, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to organize upcoming meetings by reviewing and finalizing the agendas for April 21st and April 28th, 2026. The council decided which new items to add to these agendas and set specific times for certain topics to be discussed. The council also reviewed public records from early April and checked on VEBA Advisory Committee matters.
keyTopics: Finalized April 21, 2026 meeting agenda and added new items
Organized April 28, 2026 draft agenda with scheduled discussion times
Reviewed public records from April 2-9, 2026
Assessed VEBA Advisory Committee (employee benefits) matters

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to discuss the city's spending plans for the next fiscal year, including what construction and infrastructure projects they want to fund. The council also formally recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and volunteers in the community.
keyTopics: Next year's capital improvement budget and construction projects
Teen Month proclamation
National Volunteer Week recognition

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 14, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Housing and Human Services Commission held a meeting on April 14, 2026, to review previous meeting notes and introduce a new ordinance about tenant protections for renters. The main focus was updating the city's rules around how renters are treated and protected in their homes.
keyTopics: Approval of March 25, 2026 meeting minutes
New ordinance to strengthen tenant protections for renters
Updates to Chapter 19.71 of city housing rules

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 16, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a meeting on April 16, 2026 where they opened the floor for residents to speak about any city matters for up to 3 minutes each. The council also held elections to choose a new chair and vice chair, and began discussing the upcoming fiscal year budget.
keyTopics: Public comment period - residents could address the council on any local issue
Chair and Vice Chair elections - council selected new leadership
Fiscal year budget development - planning for upcoming city spending

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 covered important legal information about how residents can challenge city decisions. If you disagree with a city decision, you have 90 days from when that decision is made to file a lawsuit, according to state law.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to sue the city over decisions
Legal challenge procedures for residents
California Civil Procedure rules governing lawsuits against Santa Clara

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026 to handle routine business and make some important decisions. The main focus was reviewing how federal funding will be used for housing and community development over the next few years, looking at requests from individual council members for new projects, and possibly changing rules about how council seats get filled when they become vacant.
keyTopics: Federal housing and community development funding plan (2025-2030)
New project requests from individual council members
Changes to rules for filling empty council seats
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming meeting schedule

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a public hearing about a historic building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto. The owner is asking to officially recognize it as a more significant historic resource, which would allow them to add 5,000 square feet of new floor space as a reward for preserving and making the building earthquake-safe.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic designation upgrade from Category 4 to Category 2
5,000 sq ft bonus floor area approval for seismic safety improvements
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule and assignments
Downtown Commercial zone development with historic preservation incentives
